blocks.content: Your name is Harvist.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Your name is Harvist.
At Arkane's words, the black orb vibrated.
As the molecular bonds loosened, the black substance billowed up like smoke.
The monstrous entity born from darkness spoke in a murky voice.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: I am Harvist.
I will conduct a few tests. For now, stay inside the test tube.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: I will conduct a few tests. For now, stay inside the test tube.
Arkane pointed to a three-meter-tall glass tube.
For a human, it would be too cramped to endure even ten minutes, but for a magical creature, the concept of temporal permanence did not exist.
That night, Kanis and Arin sneaked into the laboratory.
Since they didn't have Arkane's permission, their nervousness was evident.
However, those feelings vanished the moment they discovered Harvist.
A humanoid shadow, two meters tall.
The slender waist and dynamically broad shoulders shook Kanis's heart.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: This is the master's……
The pinnacle of magical biology, perfected by the Grand Mage over 150 years.
Moreover, it was a magical creature that had inherited Arkane's memories, endowed with intelligence.
Unlike Kanis, Arin trembled as if she had seen something grotesque.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Ugh. It's really weird. I thought it would look more human-like.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: If a magical creature didn't look terrifying, who would be scared of it? To me, this form is amazing. It's combat-ready.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Still, I find it strange……
Kanis paid no attention.
If Arin disliked it, that was even better.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: 'Wait, Harvist. I will become your master.'
Arkane had said he would choose between himself and Arin as Harvist's master.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Of course, Arin was a precious friend, but there could be no concessions in this matter.
Through fair competition, he would prove his worth.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Kanis, let's go. The master might come.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, right.
Arin turned off the light and followed Kanis out.
Then, she flinched and looked back.
The explosive emotions of the vibrating Harvist reached her.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Mommy!
Arin fled the room as if running for her life and never set foot in the laboratory again after that day.
However, having spent her childhood locked in Ladum's warehouse, she couldn't leave Harvist like that.
When everyone was asleep, Arin mustered her courage and returned to the laboratory.
Harvist remained trapped in the glass tube, unchanged from before.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Are you feeling stifled?
When no answer came, Arin examined the tube's mechanisms, searching for a switch.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I can let you out for a little while if you'd like.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Why do you assume I feel stifled?
Arin flinched and stepped back.
The voice was chilling, instinctively evoking fear.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: You've been trapped here for a month already.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: It is humans who find immobility uncomfortable. I cannot perceive time. I could remain trapped like this forever, and it wouldn't matter.
Arin tilted her head skeptically.
The wavelength she sensed from Harvist was filled with suppressed and stifled energy.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: ……But maybe you're right.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Did something occur to you?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Well. Curiosity, I suppose? That's not quite the right word. From the moment of my existence, I was designed to learn. But now, that's impossible.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: That's exactly what feeling stifled means.
Harvist fell into thought.
Was that truly the case?

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: How can you read my emotions?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Hehe. It just turned out this way. I've lived in Ladum for a long time.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Ladum. The slums of Bashka, the capital of Tormia. A high-level concealment facility with extreme population fluctuations. Presumed to be a den for displaced demi-humans who lost their territories on the continent.
Even Arin, who had lived in Ladum, was hearing this for the first time.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Harvist, you know a lot.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: What information do you have?

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Are you asking to converse?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I see. Conversation is the only option, then.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: I currently lack a master to share my mind with.
Arin talked with Harvist all night long.
For her, who had spent most of her childhood alone, it was the first time she had ever had such a long conversation with someone.
After two months of experiments, Harvist was finally granted freedom.
Arkane opened the glass tube.
But Harvist still didn't move.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Come out. You will resonate with Kanis.
However, you won't be bound by the constraints of subordination.
I'll explain the reasons in due time.
Harvist remained silent.
For a magical creature with superhuman computational speed, the delayed response was unexpected.
Prompting Arkane to turn his head.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Why? Is there something holding you back?

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Why Kanis?
Hmm. Do you dislike the idea?
It's strange that you, who haven't even formed a mental resonance, already have preferences.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: I understand there's no merit in me, who inherited your memories, being subordinated to you.
But Kanis is stubborn and prideful.
He's unlikely to heed my advice.
Such traits would be a weakness in battle.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Of course, that's true.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: On the other hand, Arin is different.
She lived in Ladum for ten years and never met anyone but Kanis.
As a result, her mental sensitivity is incredibly sharp.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Arin is out of the question.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: That child is too soft-hearted.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: I don't understand.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: First Sight outweighs any other shortcomings.
With her, my abilities could be maximized.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Arin is not an option.
You will go to Kanis.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Kekekekeke!
Harvist, plummeting, twitched.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: So that's how it was, Arkane.
Only in the final moments of life did he realize it.
The reason Arkane had abandoned Arin, who possessed exceptional magical talent, and stubbornly chosen Kanis instead.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: 'You were just as stubborn.'
Harvist's fall carried no weight.
That made it all the more pitiful.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Harvist!
Kanis rushed to Harvist.
The creature's body, crumpled like thin paper, was riddled with holes, making it painful to look at.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Why! Why did you do this!

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: I wouldn't have cared if I died!

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: If you were going to do this, you shouldn't have betrayed me in the first place!

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Why!

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Keke. How should I know?
Kanis clenched his teeth as if realizing something.
Magical creatures… just do what their masters want.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Harvist……
Kanis's head dropped.
He felt resentful and wronged, wishing he could just die.
But part of him also wanted to live.
Why wouldn't he?
That was human nature.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Harvist. I……
Harvist didn't mind.
Magical creatures were designed to do what their masters wanted.
Death wasn't shocking because of that.
Instead, he was more intrigued by Shironé's combat prowess, which had overpowered his and Kanis's combined efforts.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Hmm. Strange magic.
But it wasn't just magic that overwhelmed us.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: The opponent was undoubtedly strong.
There was no hesitation in subduing others.
Yet, they weren't aggressive by nature.
Rather, their battle style was ruthlessly result-oriented, resembling that of a magical creature.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Emotions recover at an incredible speed.
Likely due to superhuman insight.
The time from problem to solution is so short that they appear unshakable.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: Keke. Arkane, you're in for a rough time.
Arkane's expression was grim.
Neither this one nor that one would move as he wished.
Feeling disgusted by the current situation, he slowly stepped forward and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Pathetic, Kanis.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Even if you disgrace my face……

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Shut up.
Shironé cut him off.
The violent tone, unusual for him, made his friends' eyes widen.
In contrast, Arkane turned his head with interest.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Kekeke! Such a spirited child.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: How about this? I'll make you an offer……

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: I told you to shut up.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Can't you hear me?
This time, even Arkane's face stiffened.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: I'm barely holding back as it is.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: So shut up.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: One more word, and I'll blast you to pieces.
Shironé's body glowed with a faint red light.
A laser.
Its power had already been demonstrated.
If it could make Harvist, specialized in energy absorption, explode from energy overload, even a Grand Mage wouldn't stand a chance.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What's he trying to do?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: If this keeps up, something terrible will happen!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It'll be fine.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Teacher.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Shouldn't we stop him now?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Arkane is in a tricky situation.
He's at a disadvantage in compatibility.
If Shironé's magic focuses energy, the current Arkane can't block it.
Now that there's a chance he might get hurt, he won't act recklessly.
Shironé knows that.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Teacher.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Even if that were true, only Shironé could dare to act so boldly against a Grand Mage.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Fine.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: As you are now, you might overpower me.
For a moment, the surroundings fell silent.
It was hard to believe such words had come from a Grand Mage.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: But it wasn't born from cowardice.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: He even smiled leisurely.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Red wavelength.
It seems to vibrate particles, making it a natural counter to dark magic.
Moreover, I'm exhausted, so stopping you would be quite difficult.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: What do you think?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Hearing this from a Grand Mage.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Magic is fun, isn't it?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: It's neither fair nor bound by rules.
Without limits, it's unpredictable.
A meteor mage capable of obliterating a city could die at the hands of a petty mercenary mage.
That's the world of magic.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: So even if I lose to you, it wouldn't be strange.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: So what's your point?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Are you saying you'll atone for your sins?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: You could take it that way, but……
Black smoke rose from Arkane's body.
The whites of his eyes were visible, and his expression was ghostly.
A blade-like killing intent exploded outward, spreading in all directions.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Kekeke!
Shironé instinctively stepped back.
This wasn't about skill or level—it was pure fighting spirit.
The aura of death that only one who had crossed countless battlefields could emit.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: No matter how favorable the compatibility, it's useless if you can't win.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: So, what do you say?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Will you face me?
For the first time, Shironé's lips, which had been so confident, fell silent.
Honestly, he didn't feel like fighting.
Arkane's fighting spirit was that overwhelming.

blocks.speaker: Harvist
blocks.content: 'As expected of the real deal.'
He had persistently exploited weaknesses, but the moment they vanished, he switched tactics instantly.
He had no stubbornness.
His thoughts flowed like water, and his vision was open in all directions.
It was a glimpse of why Kanis had lost.
